title: Broadcast signed Ethereum transaction
summary: Submit a hex-serialized signed transaction through Ethereum JSON-RPC.

title: Broadcast signed Ethereum transaction
summary: Post an eth_sendRawTransaction request from a backend worker.
code:
body := `{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"eth_sendRawTransaction","params":["` + signedTxHex + `"]}`
req, _ := http.NewRequest("POST", rpcURL, strings.NewReader(body))
req.Header.Set("content-type", "application/json")
resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
if err != nil { return err }
defer resp.Body.Close()
if resp.StatusCode >= 300 { return fmt.Errorf("rpc status %s", resp.Status) }

title: Broadcast Bitcoin raw transaction
summary: Call Bitcoin Core RPC with a signed transaction hex.

title: Broadcast Bitcoin raw transaction
summary: Submit signed transaction hex to Bitcoin Core RPC.
code:
payload := fmt.Sprintf(`{"jsonrpc":"1.0","id":"ckg","method":"sendrawtransaction","params":["%s"]}`, signedTxHex)
req, _ := http.NewRequest("POST", rpcURL, strings.NewReader(payload))
req.SetBasicAuth(user, pass)
req.Header.Set("content-type", "text/plain")
resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
if err != nil { return err }
defer resp.Body.Close()

title: Broadcast Solana signed transaction
summary: Serialize a signed transaction and submit base64 bytes through sendTransaction.

title: Broadcast Solana signed transaction
summary: Submit base64 transaction bytes through Solana JSON-RPC.
code:
encoded := base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(signedTxBytes)
body := fmt.Sprintf(`{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"sendTransaction","params":["%s",{"encoding":"base64"}]}`, encoded)
resp, err := http.Post(rpcURL, "application/json", strings.NewReader(body))
if err != nil { return err }
defer resp.Body.Close()

title: Query ETH balance snippet
summary: Minimal fetch-based JSON-RPC example for calling eth_getBalance.
code: await fetch(rpcUrl, { method: 'POST', headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify({ jsonrpc: '2.0', id: 1, method: 'eth_getBalance', params: [address, 'latest'] }) });

title: Substrate raw extrinsic template

code:

  "address": "<ss58-address>",
  "call": "<scale-encoded-call>",
  "era": "<mortal-or-immortal-era>",
  "nonce": "<account-nonce>",
  "tip": "0",
  "specVersion": "<runtime-spec-version>",
  "transactionVersion": "<runtime-tx-version>",
  "genesisHash": "<chain-genesis-hash>",
  "blockHash": "<recent-block-hash>"
}

summary: Decode ASN.1 DER ECDSA output into fixed-width r and s values, then derive recovery id v for Ethereum-style serialized transactions.

title: Turnkey raw payload signing pattern

summary: Submit exact digest bytes to Turnkey with explicit encoding and hash mode.

title: Turnkey raw payload signing
summary: Post a sign_raw_payload activity from a Go backend.

metadata.embedding_input:
Turnkey raw payload signing
Go
Post a sign_raw_payload activity from a Go backend.
req := SignRawPayloadRequest{
  OrganizationID: orgID,
  SignWith: walletAccountID,
  Payload: hex.EncodeToString(digest),
  Encoding: "PAYLOAD_ENCODING_HEXADECIMAL",
  HashFunction: "HASH_FUNCTION_NO_OP",
}
resp, err := turnkey.SignRawPayload(ctx, req)
if err != nil { return err }
r, s, v := resp.R, resp.S, resp.V

title: Bad signature diagnostic
summary: Debug path for invalid transaction signatures caused by mismatched serialization, hashing, domain separation, or signer output normalization.
code:
1. Reconstruct the unsigned signing payload from chain metadata.
2. Hex-dump every field before serialization.
3. Compare serialized preimage against a known SDK output.
4. Verify hash function and KMS MessageType/encoding.
5. Decode signature output and check r/s/v or signature byte format.
6. Recompute sender/public key from signature before broadcasting.

metadata.embedding_input:
Bad signature diagnostic
text
Debug path for invalid transaction signatures caused by mismatched serialization, hashing, domain separation, or signer output normalization.
1. Reconstruct the unsigned signing payload from chain metadata.
2. Hex-dump every field before serialization.
3. Compare serialized preimage against a known SDK output.
4. Verify hash function and KMS MessageType/encoding.
5. Decode signature output and check r/s/v or signature byte format.
6. Recompute sender/public key from signature before broadcasting.
